teamObjective.explanation: As the opening leader with a partner near finish (only 2 cards left), winning this trick gives our team control of the table and maximizes the chance to help partner go out first.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner is close to finishing with only 2 cards remaining; their exact cards are unknown, but they likely have a mix of small and possibly one higher or trump card.

opponentBelief.summary: Opponents have unseen cards and, given my hand is all low non-trump diamonds, they likely possess higher diamonds, trumps, or both that can beat a single lead.
evidence: my private hand contains only ranks 3-6 of diamonds with no trumps
playedCardSummary shows no cards have been played yet

actionRationale.primaryReason: Leading the 6 of diamonds gives the highest probability of winning the trick among the legal single-card leads. Maintaining the lead is valuable because partner has only 2 cards and may finish soon; controlling the table lets me choose favorable leads or play around partner.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: The 3 is the weakest card and almost certainly loses to any higher diamond or trump, surrendering control immediately without gaining information.
Only marginally stronger than the 3 and still very likely to be beaten; it does not materially improve the chance of winning the trick.
Better than 3 or 4, but still inferior to the 6 for winning the trick while all four cards are equally vulnerable to trumps and higher non-trump cards.

risks: An opponent may have a higher diamond or any trump and win the trick
If partner has only low diamonds they may be forced to play a card they wanted to keep
Winning with the 6 still leaves me with three weak cards that may be hard to dispose of later
riskAssessment.mitigation: Playing the strongest available single maximizes the immediate chance to retain the lead; future leads can then be tailored once opponents reveal more information.

notes: Opening lead with a weak four-card diamond run and no trumps; priority is table control to support a partner who is one trick away from finishing.
